instruction: What is the Yuva Nidhi Scheme?

Support for unemployed youth to aid livelihood. Level: State. Target Group: Permanent resident of Karnataka

Unemployed for at least 6 months after graduation or diploma

Not employed or self-employed

Bank account linked with Aadhaar.

instruction: What is the Anna Bhagya Scheme (Karnataka Free Rice Distribution Scheme)?
output: Anna Bhagya Scheme (Karnataka Free Rice Distribution Scheme) is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Food Security/Social Welfare. Managing Department: Department of Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: 10 kg free rice per person per month to BPL families (5 kg from Centre under NFSA + 5 kg from Karnataka govt); OR equivalent cash transfer via DBT; Recently modified to include Indira Nutrition Kit (toor dal, moong dal, sugar, salt, cooking oil) for 5 kg rice portion. Level: State. Target Group: Permanent residents of Karnataka; BPL category families; Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) or Priority Household (PHH) ration cardholders.

instruction: What is the Schedule Caste (SC) Marriage Assistance Scheme?
output: Schedule Caste (SC) Marriage Assistance Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Social Welfare / Marriage Assistance. Managing Department: Commissionerate of Social Welfare, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Rs. 50,000 financial assistance to SC married couples; Amount credited directly to joint bank account of couple; Payment in installments as per government policy. Level: State. Target Group: Both bride and groom must belong to Schedule Caste (SC) category; Permanent residents of Karnataka; Marriage held after 11-08-2015; Annual family income should not exceed Rs. 2,00,000; Application submitted within 1 year of marriage.

instruction: How can I apply for Schedule Caste (SC) Marriage Assistance Scheme?
output: Here is how you can apply for Schedule Caste (SC) Marriage Assistance Scheme: Visit Social Welfare Department website (swdservices.karnataka.gov.in) or Seva Sindhu portal (sevasindhuservices.karnataka.gov.in). Click "Simple Marriage" or "SC Marriage Assistance Scheme". Register using Aadhaar number of both bride and groom. Fill online application form with personal, marriage, and bank details. Upload scanned documents (Marriage Certificate, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Marriage photo - each file size max 1MB). Enter 15-digit RD numbers of caste and income certificates. Preview application and submit. SMS confirmation sent to registered mobile number. Print acknowledgement receipt. Application forwarded to District Social Welfare Officer for spot verification

instruction: What is the Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana (RSBY)?
output: Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana (RSBY) is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Health/Social Security. Managing Department: Department of Labour & Employment, Government of India/Karnataka. Key Benefit: Health insurance coverage up to Rs. 30,000 annual family floater basis (covers all family members collectively). Level: Central/State. Target Group: BPL unorganized sector workers; Family size up to 5 members; Below Poverty Line cardholders.

instruction: What is the Madilu Kit Scheme?
output:
Madilu Kit Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Health/Maternal Welfare. Managing Department: Department of Women and Child Development, Karnataka. Key Benefit: Madilu kit valued at ~Rs. 1,500 for government hospital deliveries; Rs. 1,000 cash for private hospital deliveries. Level: State. Target Group: Pregnant women resident of Karnataka

Must belong to BPL (Below Poverty Line), Scheduled Caste, or Scheduled Tribe category

Must deliver in a Government hospital (PHC, CHC, Taluk, District, Medical College hospitals).

instruction: What is the Karnataka Krushi Aranya Protsaha Yojane (KAPY)?
output:
Karnataka Krushi Aranya Protsaha Yojane (KAPY) is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Agriculture/Forestry/Environmental Incentive. Managing Department: Karnataka Forest Department. Key Benefit: Subsidised seedlings, cash incentives up to Rs. 125 over 3 years per surviving tree (total for full term). Level: State. Target Group: Any farmer owning agricultural land in Karnataka
Must be able to provide proof of land ownership (pahani/RTC).

instruction: How can I apply for Karnataka Krushi Aranya Protsaha Yojane (KAPY)?
output:
Here is how you can apply for Karnataka Krushi Aranya Protsaha Yojane (KAPY): Visit nearest Forest Department office before monsoon (by end of May)

Obtain and fill in the free application form

Submit required documents (attested)

Pay nominal registration fee (Rs. 10)

Registered farmer receives subsidised seedlings from government nurseries

Forest officials visit the farm annually to count surviving saplings

Incentive is paid for every surviving seedling over three years:

Year 1: Rs. 35/sapling

Year 2: Rs. 40/sapling

Year 3: Rs. 50/sapling

Incentive transferred via ECS/DBT directly to beneficiary’s bank account

instruction: What is the Karnataka Self Employment Scheme?
output:
Karnataka Self Employment Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Employment. Managing Department: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited managed by Social Welfare, Backward Classes, and Women Development departments. Key Benefit: Subsidy up to 33% of project cost or max ₹1 lakh (KMDC standard)

For CMEGP/general: 25–35% subsidy, max project cost ₹10 lakh, reduced margin money contribution for reserved categories

Direct loan + subsidy to bank-linked account. Level: State. Target Group: Resident of Karnataka
Age: 18–55 years (some variants: 21–45 for SC/ST/OBC/minorities, as per department)
Annual family income:
Rural: up to ₹81,000
Urban: up to ₹1,03,000
Minority community focus: Muslims, Christians, Sikhs, Jains, Buddhists, and Parsis under KMDC variant
For CMEGP/general: SC/ST/OBC/minorities/women/ex-servicemen/disabled eligible.

instruction: What is the Ganga Kalyana Scheme?
output:
Ganga Kalyana Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Agriculture/Irrigation. Managing Department: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation (KMDC). Key Benefit: Borewell construction or open well (Rs 1.5 to 3.5 lakh cost per beneficiary depending on location/district)

Supply and installation of pump, accessories, electrification

Direct cash subsidy for irrigation pipeline in lift irrigation cases, up to Rs 3.5 lakh for select districts, Rs 2 lakh for others. Level: State. Target Group: Must belong to eligible category: Minority (Muslim/Christian/Jain etc.), SC/ST, or specified Backward Class (Cat 1, 2A, 3A, 3B) per scheme variant

Resident of Karnataka

Small/marginal farmer (generally 1–5 acres landholding)

Family must not have benefited from similar irrigation schemes

No member of the family should be a government employee.

instruction: What is the Shrama Shakthi Loan Scheme?
output:
Shrama Shakthi Loan Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Self-Employment/Entrepreneurship/Minority Welfare. Managing Department: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDC). Key Benefit: Under this scheme, a loan of ₹50,000 will be given to persons belonging to religious minority communities.Under this scheme, religious minority community entrepreneurs will be trained in artistic and technical skills.. Level: State. Target Group: Permanent resident of Karnataka

Age: 18-55 years

Annual family income less than ₹3.5 lakh

Must belong to a religious minority community (Muslim, Sikh, Parsi, Jain, Christian, Buddhist)

Should NOT have availed loan/subsidy from KMDC in last 5 years (except Arivu Scheme)

No family member working in State/Central govt/PSU

Not a defaulter for any previous KMDC loan.

instruction: What is the Arivu Education Loan Scheme?
output:

Permanent resident of Karnataka

Family income ≤ ₹8,00,000 per annum

Admission to professional courses (MBBS, BDS, BE, BTech, MBA, MCA, etc.) via CET/NEET/KEA in recognized institutions

Not open for non-minorities or non-Karnataka residents.

The Arivu Education Loan Scheme is designed to provide assistance to Minority community students admitted to professional courses through Karnataka Examination Authority (KEA)/CET/NEET. It offers Loan from ₹50,000 up to ₹3 lakh or ₹5 lakh/year (exact limit varies by year/course)

Interest rate: 2% per annum (no collateral; low interest)

Repayment starts 6 months or 12 months after course ends; full period up to 4 years. It is managed by Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation (KMDC.

instruction: Is Arivu Education Loan Scheme a central or state government scheme?
output: Arivu Education Loan Scheme is a State-level scheme. It is managed by Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation (KMDC. It falls under the Education category.

instruction: Which government body is responsible for the Arivu Education Loan Scheme?
output: The Arivu Education Loan Scheme is managed by Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation (KMDC.

instruction: Under which category does the Arivu Education Loan Scheme fall?
output: The Arivu Education Loan Scheme falls under the Education category.

instruction: Is Arivu Education Loan Scheme a government or semi-government scheme?
output: Arivu Education Loan Scheme is a Government scheme. It is managed by Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation (KMDC.

instruction: Who is eligible for the Arivu Education Loan Scheme?
output:
The eligibility criteria for Arivu Education Loan Scheme are as follows: Student must belong to Karnataka’s state-recognized minority community (Muslim, Christian, Jain, Sikh, Parsi, Buddhist)

Permanent resident of Karnataka

Family income ≤ ₹8,00,000 per annum

Admission to professional courses (MBBS, BDS, BE, BTech, MBA, MCA, etc.) via CET/NEET/KEA in recognized institutions

Not open for non-minorities or non-Karnataka residents

instruction: How can I apply for Arivu Education Loan Scheme?
output:
Here is how you can apply for Arivu Education Loan Scheme: Visit https://kmdc.karnataka.gov.in/english or https://kmdconline.karnataka.gov.in

Select “Arivu Education Loan” tab

Register, fill details, upload required scanned documents

Submit application online

Take print, submit renewal documents yearly, pay 12% previous year’s loan at district office

Loan sanctioned and directly transferred to college account (DBT)

instruction: Summarize the Arivu Education Loan Scheme for me in a few lines.
output:
Arivu Education Loan Scheme is a State-level Karnataka government scheme managed by Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation (KMDC. Beneficiaries: Minority community students admitted to professional courses through Karnataka Examination Authority (KEA)/CET/NEET. Benefits: Loan from ₹50,000 up to ₹3 lakh or ₹5 lakh/year (exact limit varies by year/course)

Interest rate: 2% per annum (no collateral; low interest)

Repayment starts 6 months or 12 months after course ends. Application mode: Online (KMDC Online Application Management System); Renewal offline mandatory at district office (by paying 12% of previous year’s loan).

instruction: What is Arivu Education Loan Scheme in Kannada context and who benefits the most?
output:
Arivu Education Loan Scheme is a government yojane (scheme) of Karnataka. The primary beneficiaries are Minority community students admitted to professional courses through Karnataka Examination Authority (KEA)/CET/NEET. It provides: Loan from ₹50,000 up to ₹3 lakh or ₹5 lakh/year (exact limit varies by year/course)

Interest rate: 2% per annum (no collateral; low interest)

Repayment starts 6 months or 12 months after course ends; full period up to 4 years. Eligibility: Student must belong to Karnataka’s state-recognized minority community (Muslim, Christian, Jain, Sikh, Parsi, Buddhist)

Permanent resident of Karnataka

Family income ≤ ₹8,00,000 per annum

Admission to professional courses (MBBS, BDS, BE, BTech, MBA, MCA, etc.) via CET/NEET/KEA in recognized institutions

Not open for non-minorities or non-Karnataka residents

instruction: What is the Karnataka Shramashakthi Special Women Scheme?
output:
Karnataka Shramashakthi Special Women Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Women Empowerment/Self-Employment/Minority Welfare. Managing Department: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDC). Key Benefit: Collateral-free loan of ₹50,000 at 4% interest, ₹25,000 subsidy if ₹25,000 is repaid in 36 months; use for any self-employment/business/service/agro activity. Level: State. Target Group: Woman resident of Karnataka

Minority community: (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Jain, Parsi, Buddhist)

Age between 18 and 55 years

Could be unmarried, widow, or divorcee

Annual family income less than ₹3.5 lakh

Should NOT have availed benefits of other KMDC schemes in last 5 years (except Arivu Scheme)

No family member should be employed in State/Central Government or PSU.

Karnataka Shramashakthi Special Women Scheme is designed to assist Women from minority communities (unmarried/divorcee/widow) needing financial help for starting a business or self-employment. Eligibility: Woman resident of Karnataka

Minority community: (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Jain, Parsi, Buddhist)

Age between 18 and 55 years

Could be unmarried, widow, or divorcee

Annual family income less than ₹3.5 lakh

Should NOT have availed benefits of other KMDC schemes in last 5 years (except Arivu Scheme)

No family member should be employed in State/Central Government or PSU Benefits: Collateral-free loan of ₹50,000 at 4% interest, ₹25,000 subsidy if ₹25,000 is repaid in 36 months; use for any self-employment/business/service/agro activity

instruction: Summarize the Karnataka Shramashakthi Special Women Scheme for me in a few lines.
output: Karnataka Shramashakthi Special Women Scheme is a State-level Karnataka government scheme managed by Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDC). Beneficiaries: Women from minority communities (unmarried/divorcee/widow) needing financial help for starting a business or self-employment. Benefits: Collateral-free loan of ₹50,000 at 4% interest, ₹25,000 subsidy if ₹25,000 is repaid in 36 months; use for any self-employment/business/service/agro activity. Application mode: Online via KMDC’s Application Portal; printout + hard copies submitted to District Selection Panel as final step.

instruction: What is Karnataka Shramashakthi Special Women Scheme in Kannada context and who benefits the most?
output:
Karnataka Shramashakthi Special Women Scheme is a government yojane (scheme) of Karnataka. The primary beneficiaries are Women from minority communities (unmarried/divorcee/widow) needing financial help for starting a business or self-employment. It provides: Collateral-free loan of ₹50,000 at 4% interest, ₹25,000 subsidy if ₹25,000 is repaid in 36 months; use for any self-employment/business/service/agro activity. Eligibility: Woman resident of Karnataka

Minority community: (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Jain, Parsi, Buddhist)

Age between 18 and 55 years

Could be unmarried, widow, or divorcee

Annual family income less than ₹3.5 lakh

Should NOT have availed benefits of other KMDC schemes in last 5 years (except Arivu Scheme)

No family member should be employed in State/Central Government or PSU

instruction: What is the Karnataka Sikhligar's Community Development Scheme?
output: Karnataka Sikhligar's Community Development Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Community Development / Self-Employment / Business Loan / Minority Welfare. Managing Department: Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka / Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDCL). Key Benefit: A) Rs. 50,000/- loan + Rs. 50,000/- subsidy for business; B) 33% subsidy (maximum Rs. 1,50,000/-) for purchasing goods tempo/commercial vehicles; C) 33% subsidy (maximum Rs. 1,00,000/-) for self-employment loans from financial institutions. Level: State. Target Group: Belong to Sikh religious minority community, permanent resident of Karnataka, age 18-55 years, annual family income below Rs. 6,00,000/-, no family member should be state/central government employee, not availed KMDCL loan/subsidy in last 5 years (except Arivu and Foreign Education Loan schemes), not defaulter of corporation.

instruction: How can I apply for Karnataka Stipend to LAW Graduates Scheme?

 Process:
 1. Visit Social Welfare Department official website
 2. Click on "REGISTER" at bottom of page
 3. Tick selection for "Fresher" and click "Proceed"
 4. Fill required details and click "Verify Applicant Aadhaar"
 5. Using Aadhaar Card, complete registration by providing all required information
 6. After registration, SMS will be sent to applicant
 7. Login with registered credentials
 8. Select "Stipend for LAW Graduates" scheme
 9. Fill personal details, educational details, and bank account details
 10. Provide RD number of Caste Certificate
 11. Provid

instruction: What is the Karnataka Ambedkar Karmika Sahaya Hastha Scheme?
output: Karnataka Ambedkar Karmika Sahaya Hastha Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Labour Welfare / Social Security / Health Insurance / Unorganized Workers. Managing Department: Karnataka State Unorganized Workers Social Security Board / Labour Department, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Smart Card facility, Rs. 1,00,000/- compensation for permanent/partial disability, Medical reimbursement up to Rs. 50,000/-, Shrama Sammana Award (Rs. 15,000/- Gold, Rs. 10,000/- Silver, Rs. 8,000/- Bronze + medals and certificates), Rs. 1,000/- cash prize for 8 workers per district per category. Level: State. Target Group: Permanent resident of Karnataka, work in identified unorganized sectors (25 categories), age 18-60 years, not registered under ESI (Employees State Insurance) and PF (Provident Fund).

instruction: How can I apply for Karnataka Ambedkar Karmika Sahaya Hastha Scheme?

 Online Process:
 1. Visit Karnataka State Unorganized Workers Social Security Board Portal
 2. Click on registration/enrollment
 3. Enter Mobile Number and press "Send OTP"
 4. Enter six-digit OTP received on mobile number
 5. Enter Captcha Code and press "Login"
 6. Provide General and Personal information
 7. Upload requisite documents
 8. Pay registration fee of Rs. 25/- online
 9. Complete process and press Submit
 10. Authority will verify details and documents
 11. Once verified, Smart Card will be issued to workers

instruction: What is the Karnataka KSRTC Arogya Scheme?
output: Karnataka KSRTC Arogya Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Health / Health Insurance / Employee Welfare / Transport Sector. Managing Department: Karnataka State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) / Transport Department, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Cashless medical treatment at 275 hospitals and 4 diagnostic centers across Karnataka (no limit on inpatient expenses), Coverage for eye/dental/Ayurveda/Unani/Homeopathy treatments, Free cardiac check-ups for employees above 40 years (partnership with Jayadeva Institute), Rs. 1 crore accident insurance, Enhanced financial aid for critical illnesses. Level: State. Target Group: KSRTC employees (officers and staff), dependents of KSRTC employees (spouse, children, parents as per rules), All India Services and Karnataka Administrative Services Officers working on assignment with KSRTC.

output:

 1. KSRTC will issue health cards to eligible employees and dependents

instruction: What is the Social Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka / Commissionerate of Social Welfare?
output:
Social Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka / Commissionerate of Social Welfare is a Karnataka government scheme. Managing Department: Social Welfare / Marriage Assistance / Women Welfare. Key Benefit: Online application through Social Welfare Department portal: https://swdservices.karnataka.gov.in/swincentive/WRM/WRMHAhome.aspx
 
 Process:
 1. Register by entering Aadhaar number of widow and new spouse
 2. Fill personal details, contact details, and marriage details
 3. Fill bank account details
 4. Upload all mandatory documents including death certificate of previous spouse
 5. Upload any address proof of previous spouse
 6. Preview the application form
 7. Submit the application
 8. Print acknowledgement receipt for future reference
 9. Application will be verified by District Social Welfare Officer
 10. After verification, financial assistance will be transferred to beneficiary bank account
 
 Application must be submitted within 1 year of remarriage.. Level: Rs. 50,000/- financial assistance for remarriage of SC widow. Target Group: Karnataka SC Widow Re-Marriage Assistance Scheme.

instruction: What benefits does Social Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka / Commissionerate of Social Welfare provide?
output:
The Social Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka / Commissionerate of Social Welfare provides the following benefits: Online application through Social Welfare Department portal: https://swdservices.karnataka.gov.in/swincentive/WRM/WRMHAhome.aspx
 
 Process:
 1. Register by entering Aadhaar number of widow and new spouse
 2. Fill personal details, contact details, and marriage details
 3. Fill bank account details
 4. Upload all mandatory documents including death certificate of previous spouse
 5. Upload any address proof of previous spouse
 6. Preview the application form
 7. Submit the application
 8. Print acknowledgement receipt for future reference
 9. Application will be verified by District Social Welfare Officer
 10. After verification, financial assistance will be transferred to beneficiary bank account
 
 Application must be submitted within 1 year of remarriage.

instruction: What is the Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Karnataka?
output:
Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Karnataka is a Karnataka government scheme. Managing Department: Tribal Welfare / Marriage Assistance. Key Benefit: Online application through Tribal Welfare Department portal: https://twd.karnataka.gov.in/twincentive/SIM/SmHome.aspx
 
 Process:
 1. Register using Aadhaar number of both bride and groom
 2. After registration, SMS will be sent to the couple
 3. Fill personal details, contact details, and marriage details
 4. Fill bank account details
 5. Upload all mandatory documents
 6. Preview the application form
 7. Submit the application
 8. Print acknowledgement form as proof of application
 9. Application will be verified by District Tribal Welfare Officer
 10. After verification, financial assistance will be transferred to beneficiary bank account
 
 Requirements:
 - Scheme applicable within 1 year of marriage
 - Whose family income is below 2.00 lakhs
 - ST couples residing in Karnataka
 - Married after 11-08-2015
 - Inter-caste married couples not eligible for this scheme. Level: Rs. 50,000/- financial assistance for ST bride. Target Group: Karnataka Schedule Tribe Marriage Assistance Scheme (Simple Marriage).

instruction: How long does it take to get benefits under Karnataka Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme?
output:
The processing time for Karnataka Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme is not explicitly stated in the available data. The application process involves: Online application through KMDCL Online Application Management Portal: https://kmdconline.karnataka.gov.in/
 
 Process:
 1. Visit KMDCL online portal
 2. Click on "Apply Online" and enter mobile number
 3. Click Submit and verify with OTP sent to mobile
 4. Enter Aadhaar number and complete Captcha
 5. Click "Next" for Aadhaar verification
 6. Enter OTP received on Aadhaar-linked mobile number
 7. Click "Continue" and select Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme
 8. Fill applicant details, vehicle details, bank details
 9. Upload all required documents (size not exceeding limits)
 10. Check summary and preview application
 11. Submit application
 12. Print acknowledgement receipt
 13. District officer w For exact timelines, contact Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDCL) / Social Welfare Department / Backward Classes Welfare Department / Tribal Welfare Department.

instruction: Can migrants living in Karnataka apply for Karnataka Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme?
output: The eligibility for Karnataka Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme requires: Permanent resident of Karnataka, belong to religious minority/SC/ST/OBC, age 18-55 years, annual family income below Rs. 4,50,000/-, valid driving license, no family member should be central/state government employee, not benefited from KMDCL schemes in last 5 years (except Arivu Scheme). If you meet the residency and other conditions, you may be eligible. Please verify with Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDCL) / Social Welfare Department / Backward Classes Welfare Department / Tribal Welfare Department.

instruction: Summarize the Karnataka Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for me in a few lines.
output: Karnataka Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme is a State-level Karnataka government scheme managed by Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDCL) / Social Welfare Department / Backward Classes Welfare Department / Tribal Welfare Department. Beneficiaries: Unemployed youth belonging to Minority, SC, ST, and OBC communities seeking self-employment through vehicle purchase. Benefits: 50% of vehicle cost or maximum Rs. 3,00,000/- subsidy for taxi/goods vehicle approved by bank; Maximum Rs. 75,000/- subsidy for passenger auto rickshaw purchase. Application mode: Online.

instruction: What is Karnataka Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme in Kannada context and who benefits the most?
output: Karnataka Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme is a government yojane (scheme) of Karnataka. The primary beneficiaries are Unemployed youth belonging to Minority, SC, ST, and OBC communities seeking self-employment through vehicle purchase. It provides: 50% of vehicle cost or maximum Rs. 3,00,000/- subsidy for taxi/goods vehicle approved by bank; Maximum Rs. 75,000/- subsidy for passenger auto rickshaw purchase. Eligibility: Permanent resident of Karnataka, belong to religious minority/SC/ST/OBC, age 18-55 years, annual family income below Rs. 4,50,000/-, valid driving license, no family member should be central/state government employee, not benefited from KMDCL schemes in last 5 years (except Arivu Scheme)

instruction: Summarize the Permanent resident of Karnataka, belong to religious minority community, age 18-55 years, family income below Rs. 1,03,000/- per annum (urban) or Rs. 81,000/- per annum (rural), no family member should be state/central/PSU government employee, not availed KMDCL benefits in last 5 years (except Arivu Scheme) for me in a few lines.
output:
Permanent resident of Karnataka, belong to religious minority community, age 18-55 years, family income below Rs. 1,03,000/- per annum (urban) or Rs. 81,000/- per annum (rural), no family member should be state/central/PSU government employee, not availed KMDCL benefits in last 5 years (except Arivu Scheme) is a Yes-level Karnataka government scheme managed by Karnataka Vrutti Protsaha Loan Scheme. Beneficiaries: Residence proof of Karnataka, Aadhaar Card, Project Report, Passport size photo, Caste/Minority Certificate, Income Certificate, Age Proof, Bank Account Details, Self Declaration Form, Surety Self Declaration Form, Mobile Number. Benefits: State. Application mode: Online application through KMDCL Online Application Management Portal: https://kmdconline.karnataka.gov.in/
 
 Process:
 1. Visit Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation official website
 2. Click "Apply Online" and enter mobile number
 3. Click Submit to verify mobile number with OTP
 4. Enter Aadhaar number, complete Captcha, click "Next"
 5. Enter OTP sent to Aadhaar-linked mobile number
 6. Click "Continue" and allow official notice
 7. Select Vrutti Protsaha Loan Scheme from scheme list
 8. Fill personal details, contact details, and project information
 9. Provide detailed project report
 10. Upload all mandatory documents
 11. Preview application form
 12. Submit application
 13. Print acknowledgement receipt
 14. Authority will verify application and documents
 15. After approval, loan amount will be sanctioned
 16. 50% will be given as loan, 50% as subsidy
 
 Loan can be used for: Agro-based activities, Starting new business, Developing existing business, Service sector, Small scale industries, Any self-employment activity..

instruction: What is the Karnataka ST Inter Caste Marriage Assistance Scheme?
output: Karnataka ST Inter Caste Marriage Assistance Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Tribal Welfare / Marriage Assistance. Managing Department: Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Rs. 2,50,000/- if ST Man marries Non-ST woman; Rs. 3,00,000/- if ST Woman marries Non-ST man. Level: State. Target Group: ST resident of Karnataka marrying Hindu Non-ST individual, marriage on or after 07-02-2019, combined annual family income below Rs. 5,00,000/-, application within 1.5 years (18 months) of marriage.

  - Marriage Proof
  - Marriage Photo
  - Mobile Number

 7. Submit the application
 8. Print acknowledgement form as proof of application
 9. Application will be verified by District Tribal Welfare Office

instruction: How can I apply for Karnataka ST Widow Re-Marriage Assistance Scheme?
output:
Here is how you can apply for Karnataka ST Widow Re-Marriage Assistance Scheme: Online application through Tribal Welfare Department portal: https://twd.karnataka.gov.in/twincentive/WRM/WRMhaHome.aspx
 
 Process:
 1. Register by entering Aadhaar number of widow and new husband
 2. After registration, SMS will be sent to the couple
 3. Fill personal details, contact details, and marriage details
 4. Fill bank account details
 5. Upload all mandatory documents including:
  - Death certificate of previous spouse
  - Identity proof of deceased husband
  - Present marriage certificate
  - Marriage photograph
 6. Preview the application form
 7. Submit the application
 8. Print acknowledgement receipt for future reference
 9. Application will be verified by District Tribal We

instruction: What is the Shaadi Shagun Scheme for minorities (Shaadi Bhagya / Bidaai Scheme)?
output: Shaadi Shagun Scheme for minorities (Shaadi Bhagya / Bidaai Scheme) is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Social Welfare / Marriage Assistance / Minority Welfare / Women Empowerment. Managing Department: Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Rs. 50,000/- financial assistance for marriage of minority bride. Level: State. instruction: What is the Social Security Pension Scheme for construction workers?
output: Social Security Pension Scheme for construction workers is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Labour Welfare / Social Security / Pension / Construction Workers. Managing Department: Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board / Labour Department, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Monthly superannuation pension Rs. 3,000/- after age 60; Family pension 50% of pension (Rs. 1,500/-) for spouse of deceased worker. Level: State. Target Group: Registered construction worker with Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, attained 60 years of age, subscriber to fund for continuous 3+ years before turning 60, paid subscription fee of Rs. 50/- per year until 60 years, not employed continuously for 1 year.

instruction: How can I apply for Social Security Pension Scheme for construction workers?
output:
Here is how you can apply for Social Security Pension Scheme for construction workers: Application through Labour Office/Karnataka Building Workers Welfare Board:
 - Construction worker must be registered with KBOCWW Board
 - Pay annual subscription of Rs. 50/- continuously until age 60
 - Apply for pension after attaining 60 years
 - Submit application with required documents to Labour Office
 - Officials verify registration, subscription payments, and eligibility
 - After verification and approval, pension of Rs. 3,000/- per month sanctioned
 - Pension credited to bank account monthly
 
 Family Pension: Spouse of deceased registered construction worker drawing pension is eligible for family pension which is 50% of pension amount.
 
 Important: Worker ceases to be beneficiary

instruction: What is the Vrutti Protsaha (Self Employment Loan Scheme)?
output: Vrutti Protsaha (Self Employment Loan Scheme) is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Employment / Self-Employment / Business Loan / Financial Assistance. Managing Department: Minorities Welfare Department / Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDCL). Key Benefit: Rs. 1,00,000/- (Rs. 50,000/- loan + Rs. 50,000/- subsidy). Level: State. Target Group: Permanent resident of Karnataka, religious minority, age 18-55 years, family income below Rs. 1,03,000/- (urban) or Rs. 81,000/- (rural), no family member in govt service, not availed KMDCL benefits in last 5 years.

instruction: What is the Subsidy Loans for Sericulture & Agriculture Sectors?
output: Subsidy Loans for Sericulture & Agriculture Sectors is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Sericulture / Agriculture / Industry Development / Minority Welfare / Financial Assistance. Managing Department: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDCL) / Department of Sericulture, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: KMDC: Rs. 2 lakh loan (Rs. 1 lakh repayable + Rs. 1 lakh subsidy) for silk reelers. State Programs: Rs. 4,125 to Rs. 7.5 lakh subsidies for various sericulture activities (mulberry, equipment, machinery, infrastructure). Level: State (KMDC for minorities) + Central-State (CDP for all farmers). Target Group: For Sericulture (KMDC): Religious minority, permanent Karnataka resident, age 18-55, engaged in silk reeling/spinning, income below Rs. 6 lakh, no family member in govt service. For Agriculture (Dept of Sericulture): Small/marginal/woman/SC/ST sericulturists with mulberry gardens.

instruction: Are there government schemes for farmers in Karnataka? Tell me about Subsidy Loans for Sericulture & Agriculture Sectors.
output: Subsidy Loans for Sericulture & Agriculture Sectors is a Karnataka government scheme for farmers. Eligibility: For Sericulture (KMDC): Religious minority, permanent Karnataka resident, age 18-55, engaged in silk reeling/spinning, income below Rs. 6 lakh, no family member in govt service. For Agriculture (Dept of Sericulture): Small/marginal/woman/SC/ST sericulturists with mulberry gardens Benefits: KMDC: Rs. 2 lakh loan (Rs. 1 lakh repayable + Rs. 1 lakh subsidy) for silk reelers. State Programs: Rs. 4,125 to Rs. 7.5 lakh subsidies for various sericulture activities (mulberry, equipment, machinery, infrastructure)

instruction: What is the Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans?
output: Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Employment / Self-Employment / Transport / Vehicle Subsidy / Artisan Development. Managing Department: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDCL) / Social Welfare Department / Backward Classes Welfare Department / Tribal Welfare Department / Karnataka Vimukt Jathi Development Corporation. Key Benefit: SC/ST: 75% of vehicle cost or maximum Rs. 4,00,000/-; Minority/OBC: 50% of vehicle cost or maximum Rs. 3,00,000/-; Auto rickshaw: Rs. 75,000/- subsidy. Level: State. Target Group: Permanent resident of Karnataka, belong to religious minority/SC/ST/OBC/Category 3B artisans, age 18-55 years, annual family income below Rs. 4,50,000/-, valid driving license, no family member in central/state govt service, not benefited from corporation schemes in last 5 years (except Arivu).

instruction: Are there any important points or conditions I should know about Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans?
output: Here are important details about Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans: Multiple corporations implementing based on community: KMDCL for minorities, Social Welfare for SC, BC Welfare for OBC, Tribal Welfare for ST, KVJDC for Category 3B artisans. Vehicle purchased must be for commercial use only. Scheme promotes self-employment and reduces unemployment among marginalized communities. Bank loan facility available for remaining vehicle cost.

output: Key details that distinguish Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans: Multiple corporations implementing based on community: KMDCL for minorities, Social Welfare for SC, BC Welfare for OBC, Tribal Welfare for ST, KVJDC for Category 3B artisans. Vehicle purchased must be for commercial use only. Scheme promotes self-employment and reduces unemployment among marginalized communities. Bank loan facility available for remaining vehicle cost.

instruction: Summarize the Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans for me in a few lines.
output: Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans is a State-level Karnataka government scheme managed by Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDCL) / Social Welfare Department / Backward Classes Welfare Department / Tribal Welfare Department / Karnataka Vimukt Jathi Development Corporation. Beneficiaries: Unemployed youth from Minority, SC, ST, OBC, and artisan communities seeking self-employment through vehicle purchase for commercial use. Benefits: SC/ST: 75% of vehicle cost or maximum Rs. 4,00,000/-; Minority/OBC: 50% of vehicle cost or maximum Rs. 3,00,000/-; Auto rickshaw: Rs. 75,000/- subsidy. Application mode: Online.

instruction: What is Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans in Kannada context and who benefits the most?
output: Swavalambi Sarathi Scheme for Artisans is a government yojane (scheme) of Karnataka. The primary beneficiaries are Unemployed youth from Minority, SC, ST, OBC, and artisan communities seeking self-employment through vehicle purchase for commercial use. It provides: SC/ST: 75% of vehicle cost or maximum Rs. 4,00,000/-; Minority/OBC: 50% of vehicle cost or maximum Rs. 3,00,000/-; Auto rickshaw: Rs. 75,000/- subsidy. Eligibility: Permanent resident of Karnataka, belong to religious minority/SC/ST/OBC/Category 3B artisans, age 18-55 years, annual family income below Rs. 4,50,000/-, valid driving license, no family member in central/state govt service, not benefited from corporation schemes in last 5 years (except Arivu)

instruction: What is the Samrudhi Scheme?
output: Samrudhi Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Agriculture / Farm Loan / Subsidy. Managing Department: Department of Agriculture, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Agricultural loan Rs. 50,000-2 lakhs with 25-50% subsidy, Soft interest rate (4-6%), Repayment period 3-5 years, Input cost coverage (seeds, fertilizer, equipment). Level: State/Block. Target Group: Karnataka domicile farmers, agricultural land holders, engaged in farming, annual income <Rs. 5 lakhs, all categories.

instruction: What is the Gelathi Scheme?
output: Gelathi Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Women Empowerment / Self-Employment / Loan. Managing Department: Karnataka State Women Development Corporation (KSWDC). Key Benefit: Business loan Rs. 1-5 lakhs with 25-50% subsidy, Margin money Rs. 10,000-25,000 grant (no repayment), Soft interest 4-6% (concessional rate), Skill training support (2-3 weeks), Mentorship program (6 months), Collateral security requirement: 50% of loan amount. instruction: How can I apply for Gruha Arogya Yojana?
output: Here is how you can apply for Gruha Arogya Yojana: Method 1 - Online: Visit https://arogya.karnataka.gov.in, click "Beneficiary Registration", enter Aadhaar number, fill personal details, submit. Hospital will receive auto-generated registration ID. Method 2 - Offline: Visit empaneled hospital with identity proof. Hospital staff will register you in Arogya Yojana system. No separate form required. Cashless treatment activated immediately after registration. Processing time: 5-10 minutes (online), real-time (offline at hospital).

instruction: What is the Samagra Mathru Arogya Palana Scheme?
output: Samagra Mathru Arogya Palana Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Health / Maternal Health / Prenatal Care. Managing Department: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Free prenatal care (regular checkups, monitoring), Free ultrasound and blood tests, Free delivery at government facility (vaginal or cesarean), Nutritional support (supplementary nutrition Rs. 500-600/month for 6 months), Health counseling and nutrition education, Postnatal care for mother and newborn (free for 42 days). Level: State. Target Group: Pregnant women, all income levels, Karnataka residents, all age groups (15-49 years), all categories, first pregnancy to 2-3 children.

instruction: How can I apply for Samagra Mathru Arogya Palana Scheme?
output: Here is how you can apply for Samagra Mathru Arogya Palana Scheme: Step 1: Register at nearest Primary Health Center (PHC) or Anganwadi center with pregnancy proof (ultrasound report or medical certificate). Step 2: Receive pregnancy ID card. Step 3: Attend free prenatal checkups at PHC (monthly in first/second trimester, bi-weekly in third trimester). Step 4: Receive nutritional supplements (iron tablets, folic acid). Step 5: At delivery time, go to government hospital for free delivery assistance. All checkups and delivery done free. Visit PHC during office hours: Mon-Fri, 10 AM - 4 PM.

instruction: What is the Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship Scheme?
output: Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Education / Scholarship / Overseas Studies. Managing Department: Department of Social Welfare, Government of Karnataka. instruction: How can I apply for Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship Scheme?
output: Here is how you can apply for Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship Scheme: Step 1: Create account on SWD portal (https://swdservices.karnataka.gov.in) using Aadhaar. Step 2: Fill "Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship" application form with all personal and academic details. Step 3: Upload scanned documents (domicile, caste, income, admission letter, transcripts). Step 4: Submit application (before deadline - typically April 30 for next academic year). Step 5: Shortlisted candidates notified via email/SMS. Step 6: Selected scholars attend in-person interview at SWD office (Bengaluru). Step 7: Merit list finalized and scholarship amount transferred to university account OR candidate bank account. Timeline: 3-4 months from application to final selection. Interview is manda

instruction: Summarize the Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship Scheme for me in a few lines.
output: Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship Scheme is a State-level Karnataka government scheme managed by Department of Social Welfare, Government of Karnataka. Beneficiaries: Meritorious Karnataka students for overseas higher education (UG/PG/PhD). Benefits: Full/partial tuition fee scholarship (covers 50-100% of university fees up to Rs. 15-25 lakhs), Monthly living allowance Rs. 1-2 lakhs per annum, Travel cost support (air ticket reimbursement up to Rs. Application mode: Online.

instruction: What is Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship Scheme in Kannada context and who benefits the most?
output: Prabhuddha Overseas Scholarship Scheme is a government yojane (scheme) of Karnataka. The primary beneficiaries are Meritorious Karnataka students for overseas higher education (UG/PG/PhD). It provides: Full/partial tuition fee scholarship (covers 50-100% of university fees up to Rs. 15-25 lakhs), Monthly living allowance Rs. 1-2 lakhs per annum, Travel cost support (air ticket reimbursement up to Rs. 1 lakh), Academic material allowance, Contingency fund (Rs. 50,000 for emergencies). Eligibility: Karnataka domicile students, SC/ST/OBC/General, pursuing higher education abroad (UG/PG), minimum 60% marks in previous exam, annual family income <Rs. 8 lakhs

instruction: What is the Loan for Overseas Education Scheme?
output: Loan for Overseas Education Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Education / Education Loan / Overseas Studies. Managing Department: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDC). Key Benefit: Education loan Rs. 5-20 lakhs (based on university fees and collateral value), 0% interest rate (income <Rs. 8L, government pays interest), 5% interest rate (income Rs. 8-15L), Repayment after course completion (grace period 6 months-1 year), No processing fees (waived for income <Rs. 8L). Level: State. Target Group: Minority community students (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Parsi, Jain, Buddhist), Karnataka domicile, admission in foreign university, minimum 60% marks, annual family income <Rs. 8 lakhs (0% interest), Rs. 8-15 lakhs (5% interest).

instruction: How can I apply for Loan for Overseas Education Scheme?
output: Here is how you can apply for Loan for Overseas Education Scheme: Method 1 - Online: Visit https://kmdcl.karnataka.gov.in, click "Apply for Education Loan", register with email/mobile, fill application form with admission and collateral details, upload all documents. KMDC reviews and sends acknowledgment. Method 2 - Offline: Visit KMDC office (Bengaluru or regional offices), submit printed application with original documents. Step 1: Submit application with admission letter and property documents. Step 2: KMDC verifies minority status and income (15-20 days). Step 3: Property valuation by KMDC surveyor (5-10 days). Step 4: Loan sanctioning based on property value. Step 5: Loan approval and disbursement (30-45 days total). Processing time: 45-60 days. Appli

instruction: How long does it take to get benefits under Loan for Overseas Education Scheme?

The application process involves: Method 1 - Online: Visit https://kmdcl.karnataka.gov.in, click "Apply for Education Loan", register with email/mobile, fill application form with admission and collateral details, upload all documents. instruction: What is the Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST?
output: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Education / Scholarship / Pre-Matric (Classes 1-10). Managing Department: Department of Social Welfare, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Monthly scholarship Rs. 500-1500 (varies by class), Book and uniform allowance Rs. 1000-3000 per annum, Hostel grant Rs. 5000-10000 per annum (for hosteled students), Transportation allowance for distant schools. Level: State. Target Group: Students classes 1-10, SC/ST category, Karnataka domicile, annual family income <Rs. 2.5 lakhs, studying in government/aided/private schools.

instruction: How can I apply for Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST?
output: Here is how you can apply for Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST: Method 1 - Online: Visit https://ssp.karnataka.gov.in, click "Pre-Matric Scholarship", select class and category (SC/ST), enter Aadhaar number, fill personal and academic details, upload caste certificate (e-certificate preferred), submit. Scholarship status visible online. Method 2 - Offline: Students register at school during academic year. School principal submits bulk scholarship list to SSP portal. Online registration recommended for faster processing. Scholarship credited automatically to linked bank account (monthly). Processing time: 15-30 days.

Required documents: Aadhaar, SC/ST caste certificate (original or e-certificate from NSCF), Income certificate (from tahasildar), Domicile certificate (if not in Aadhaar), School enrollment certificate (from school principal), Bank account details (linked to Aadhaar), Ration card (for verification, optional). Please contact Department of Social Welfare, Government of Karnataka for guidance on missing documents.

instruction: Is there a scholarship scheme for students from Karnataka? Tell me about Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST.
output: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST is an education/scholarship scheme in Karnataka. Eligibility: Students classes 1-10, SC/ST category, Karnataka domicile, annual family income <Rs. 2.5 lakhs, studying in government/aided/private schools Benefits: Monthly scholarship Rs. 500-1500 (varies by class), Book and uniform allowance Rs. 1000-3000 per annum, Hostel grant Rs. 5000-10000 per annum (for hosteled students), Transportation allowance for distant schools

instruction: What is the Post-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST?
output: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Education / Scholarship / Post-Matric (Classes 11+). Managing Department: Department of Social Welfare, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Monthly scholarship Rs. 2000-5000 (varies by course), Hostel grant Rs. 10,000-20,000 per annum, Book and examination fee grant Rs. 3000-5000 per annum, Special allowance for professional courses (engineering, medical, law). Level: State. Target Group: Students class 11+ (including graduation/PG), SC/ST category, Karnataka domicile, annual family income <Rs. 2.5 lakhs, 50% marks in previous exam, studying in government/aided/private colleges.

instruction: How can I apply for Post-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST?
output: Here is how you can apply for Post-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST: Method 1 - Online: Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in, click "SC/ST Post-Matric Scholarship", select course level (12/UG/PG), enter Aadhaar, fill academic and personal details, upload caste certificate and college enrollment proof, submit. Scholarship status tracked online. Method 2 - Offline: Register at college during admission or within 60 days of enrollment. College principal submits scholarship list to SSP portal. Online application recommended for faster processing. Scholarship credited to bank account monthly or in 2-3 installments per year. Processing: 30-45 days after verification.

instruction: What is the Vidyasiri Scholarship Scheme?
output: Vidyasiri Scholarship Scheme is a Karnataka government scheme. Category: Education / Scholarship / OBC (Food & Accommodation). Managing Department: Department of Backwards Classes Welfare, Government of Karnataka. Key Benefit: Monthly stipend Rs. 1500 for 10 months (May-February, Rs. 15,000 total per annum) for food and accommodation expenses ONLY (not tuition), Direct bank transfer, Available for all post-matric courses (Class 11-12, UG, PG). Level: State. Target Group: Post-matric OBC students (Category 1/2A/3A/3B), Karnataka domicile, annual family income <Rs. 1-2.5 lakhs (varies by category), minimum 40-60% marks (varies by category), rural students (5+ km from college).

instruction: How can I apply for Vidyasiri Scholarship Scheme?
output: Here is how you can apply for Vidyasiri Scholarship Scheme: Method 1 - Online (KarePass Portal): Visit https://karepass.cgg.gov.in, click "Student Services" → "Vidyasiri Scholarship", login with Aadhaar/ID, select college and OBC category, fill application form, upload caste certificate (both old and new formats accepted), upload income certificate and college enrollment proof, submit. Status tracked online. Method 2 - Offline: Register at college during admission or within 90 days of enrollment. College submits list to portal. Processing time: 30-45 days for verification and approval. Stipend credited to bank account in 2-3 installments per year (typically May, August, November). Eligibility re-verified each year based on 75% attendance.

instruction: Is there a scholarship scheme for students from Karnataka? Tell me about Vidyasiri Scholarship Scheme.
